title: Add verdict_explanation field to capture LLM reasoning
body:
## Summary
This PR adds a `verdict_explanation` field to agent results to capture brief LLM-generated explanations of why agents chose their verdicts. This helps human reviewers understand what to focus on when reviewing flagged PRs.

## Key Changes

- **Database Migration**: Added `verdict_explanation` column (nullable Text) to `agent_results` table via Alembic migration with idempotent column existence check
- **Data Model**: Added `verdict_explanation: str | None` field to `AgentResult` dataclass
- **Agent Prompt**: Updated base agent prompt to require `verdict_explanation` for "warn" and "flag_human" verdicts (1-2 sentence summaries of reasoning and review focus)
- **Agent Parsing**: Modified `_parse_response()` to extract and store `verdict_explanation` from LLM responses
- **Storage Layer**: Updated `save_review_result()` and `_review_to_dict()` to persist and retrieve `verdict_explanation`
- **Dashboard UI**: 
  - Added "Why This PR Needs Human Review" section to display override reasons
  - Added "Review focus" subsection under agent verdict cards (shown for warn/flag_human verdicts)
  - Properly escaped and formatted explanation text in HTML
- **Tests**: Added tests to verify explanation preservation and null default behavior

## Implementation Details

- The `verdict_explanation` field is optional (nullable) to maintain backward compatibility
- LLM is instructed to set explanation to null for "pass" verdicts and provide 1-2 sentence summaries for "warn"/"flag_human"
